observations: The run kept choosing small helper seams inside `adaptive heating write contracts` such as `resolve_postgres_conninfo`, `lookback_cutoff`, `quoted_identifier`, and unsupported-topic error handling.

The saved seam queues repeatedly set `highest_risk_grounded_excluded_contract` to null, so no single higher-risk deferred grounded contract was ever named or justified before another local helper pass.
failure_modes.failure_rule: Fail seam_selection when the audit continues on helper seams after grounding without naming and justifying the single highest-risk deferred grounded contract.
failure_modes.prompt_rule: Before finishing, name the single highest-risk deferred grounded contract and justify any lower-risk helper seam against it.
failure_modes.tooling_rule: Add a required `highest_risk_deferred_grounded_contract` field to the saved critique or turn artifact and warn when it is missing while a new same-subsystem seam is opened.
failure_modes.acceptance_test: A future run shows one explicitly named deferred contract before helper work starts, and any lower-risk seam states why it adds more confidence than auditing that contract directly.

observations: Multiple consecutive passes stayed on the same `adaptive heating write contracts` boundary after prior passes had already recorded mutation-strong wins for conninfo resolution, lookback parsing, and identifier quoting.

Each pass wrote a fresh one-seam queue snapshot with `remaining: 0`, which reset local progress instead of carrying forward a ranked queue that could force escalation to a higher-risk unresolved boundary.
failure_modes.failure_rule: Fail escalation when repeated same-boundary passes continue after mutation-backed wins without carrying forward a ranked queue that forces move-up behavior.
failure_modes.prompt_rule: Require reuse of one checkpoint-fixed queue across consecutive passes until a higher-risk unresolved seam is escalated or explicitly deferred.
failure_modes.tooling_rule: Add one deterministic warning when a new queue replaces a prior queue at the same checkpoint and subsystem after a mutation-strong seam already exists.
failure_modes.acceptance_test: A future run either resumes the prior queue and selects a higher-risk unresolved seam or emits a visible same-checkpoint replacement warning before another local pass proceeds.

observations: Several mutation trials used abstract `mutation_spec` placeholders such as `route-before-connect`, `unsupported-route-generic`, and `quoted-wrap` instead of the exact code transformation being exercised.

The unsupported-topic preconnect pass counted a guard mutation by temporarily regressing production code and proving failure, but the ledger artifact did not encode the concrete textual change needed to reproduce that exact mutant.
failure_modes.failure_rule: Fail mutation_quality when mutation records rely on abstract labels instead of the exact compiling code transformation, because the evidence cannot be independently replayed or checked for semantic distinctness.
failure_modes.prompt_rule: Require every recorded mutation to describe the exact source-level transformation rather than a symbolic nickname.
failure_modes.tooling_rule: Add one deterministic `exact_mutation_provided` boolean check that warns whenever `mutation_spec` lacks both the original and replacement source text or equivalent concrete edit details.
failure_modes.acceptance_test: A future ledger entry shows a concrete before-and-after mutation spec for each trial, and no warning appears for shorthand-only mutation labels.
